behaviors.description: The traveling-salesman heuristic produces a route that is no more than 1.5x the length of the optimal route for inputs up to 20 stops.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: the failure boundary is the heuristic’s own algorithmic output quality, not a module handoff. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: no OS/network/subprocess is required; synthetic coordinate sets are sufficient. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: no AI dependency. Rejection gate: higher levels are disqualified by the behavior text focusing only on route-length ratio vs optimal, which is local algorithm logic.
behaviors.test_description: Test the heuristic function directly with fixed coordinate fixtures (and seeded random small cases). Compute optimal tour length with an exact solver on tractable subsets, then assert heuristic_length/optimal_length <= 1.5 for cases up to 20 stops.

behaviors.description: The delivery time window estimates are computed correctly from the distance between consecutive stops and the configured average speed for the road type.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "computed correctly from the distance between consecutive stops and the configured average speed for the road type." Slot B: decisive boundary signal = handoff across modules (distance computation + road-type speed config + estimator). Slot C: if lowered to Unit, a real miss is incorrect wiring of road-type-to-speed lookup while each function independently passes. Integration brake: no real OS/network/subprocess, no AI output dependence, no multi-agent chain. Rejection gate: System is disqualified because behavior does not require real HTTP/OS effects; Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because no model decision is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real local modules for segment-distance provider, road-type speed configuration, and time estimator. Run a multi-stop route fixture with mixed road types and assert each window and total timing matches expected calculations, including per-segment speed selection.

behaviors.description: The shift-hours validator rejects a route whose total estimated time exceeds the driver's remaining shift hours, returning the excess amount.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this behavior is a local comparison/arithmetic rule on provided durations and shift hours. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: no external infrastructure needed. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate: higher levels are disqualified by the behavior text being a pure validator contract (reject + excess amount) with no cross-module or infrastructure dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Call the validator function with representative totals and remaining-shift inputs, including edge cases (equal, slightly over, large over), and assert rejection flag plus exact excess-time value.

behaviors.description: When one address in the list fails geocoding (API returns no results), the service skips that stop and optimizes the remaining addresses, reporting which addresses were skipped.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "service skips that stop and optimizes the remaining addresses, reporting which addresses were skipped." Slot B: decisive boundary signal = handoff/orchestration across geocoding result handling, filtering, optimizer input, and reporting output. Slot C: if lowered to Unit, a real miss is dropped-stop reporting diverging from optimizer input after geocode filtering. Integration brake: (a) real network is not required because deterministic "no results" can be injected via in-process fake geocoder; (b) no AI output dependence; (c) no multi-agent sequence. Rejection gate: System considered due to API mention, but disqualified by behavior text specifying only logical handling of a no-result response, not real HTTP/network behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run the service with real local orchestration modules and a fake geocoder adapter returning valid coordinates for all but one address (no results for one). Assert: skipped list contains only that address, optimizer is called/evaluated on remaining stops, and final route excludes skipped stop.

behaviors.description: Duplicate addresses in the input list are consolidated into a single stop with combined delivery items, not visited twice.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "consolidated into a single stop with combined delivery items, not visited twice." Slot B: decisive boundary signal = handoff between dedup/merge logic and downstream routing/visit generation. Slot C: if lowered to Unit, a real miss is correct dedup map output but route planner still receives unmerged stops and visits twice. Integration brake: no real OS/network/subprocess, no AI model dependence, no multi-step agentic flow. Rejection gate: System/Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because behavior is local service orchestration without real infra or model output.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the route-building pipeline with duplicate-address fixtures containing different item payloads. Assert merged stop count, combined item set/quantities, and final route visit sequence includes that address exactly once.
